# D-13 transcript deposit — sensitive-content scan (FOR REVIEW)

Generated by `scripts/collab_deposit_prep.py` on 2026-06-07. Matched strings are
masked in this report. Severity: HIGH = likely credential, resolve before any
deposit; MEDIUM = identifying but expected provenance (author identity, local
paths); LOW = informational / probable false positive. Per D-13, any redaction
of the deposited files must itself be logged.

| email address | MEDIUM | 301 | 30 |

## Analyst classification (manual review, 2026-06-07)

The first scan pass (before the suppression rules above were added) raised
four HIGH-severity match groups. Each was inspected in full context and
classified; the suppression rules encode exactly these findings:

1. `api_key assignment` (2 matches, 424fd178): the matched text is
   `api_key = get_zotero_api_key` — a tool result quoting source code of
   `scripts/` helpers; a function call, not a literal credential.
2. `Google API key` (2 matches, 5ee18e70 + 63b28d6e): `AIza...`-shaped
   substrings inside >=60-char base64 runs (embedded blob content);
   random collisions, not keys.
3. `financial/identity keyword` (5 matches, 2 files): four are quotations of
   Rubin (1987), preface — the United States Social Security Administration in
   the historical account of multiple imputation's origin; one is `/SSN+`
   inside a base64 run. None is personal financial information.
4. An earlier `health keyword` pattern that included `diagnos*` produced
   ~13,000 matches, all statistical ('diagnostic', 'diagnose the bug').
   The pattern was narrowed to clinical terms. All 34 surviving matches were
   then inspected individually: every one is quoted methodology literature
   (the fluvoxamine worked example; the UK700 psychiatric-case-management
   trial in a variable-selection paper; NHANES lipid-lowering-medication
   rates) or the word 'prescription' used rhetorically or mathematically.
   No personal health information is present.

Remaining MEDIUM categories are expected provenance, present by design of
D-13 (the author is identified; local paths identify the machine layout):

- Local Windows paths with the username `marcu` appear throughout (~35k
  matches): tool calls, file listings, configuration. Removing them would
  gut the record; D-13 anticipated path exposure. Recommend: deposit as-is.
- Email addresses (~300 matches): predominantly the author's own git/Zotero
  identities and emails quoted from acquired papers' title pages.
  Recommend: deposit as-is; the author identity is public per JAIGP norms.

No redaction is recommended by this scan. If Marcus elects any redaction,
it must be logged per D-13 (file, byte range, reason, replacement).
